channel.title: Deficiency of Vitamin D on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Deficiency of Vitamin D on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Our World in Data - Vitamin D deficiency map - 2023
item.description:
?? < 20 ng ??

Vitamin D Fortification - 2024 book chapter

item.title: Vitamin D as get older: more D and less deficiency (UK Health Care workers)
item.description:
• Vital D: A modifiable occupational risk factor of UK healthcare workers
      • Examples of related studies in VitaminDWiki

Vital D: A modifiable occupational risk factor of UK healthcare workers

PLoS One. 2024 Apr 16;19(4):e0296247. doi: 10.1371/journal.pone.0296247

James Phelan 1, Angukumar Thangamuthu 1, Srinivasagam Muthumeenal 2, Kirsteen Houston 3, Mark Everton 1, Sathyanarayana Gowda 1, Jufen Zhang 4, Rengarajan Subramanian 1

Background: The role of Vitamin D in immune function is well reported with a growing evidence base linking low levels to poorer outcomes from infectious disease. Vitamin D deficiency and insufficiency are prevalent worldwide with healthcare workers identified as a known at-risk group. Here we aim to investigate serum Vitamin D levels in a UK population of front line healthcare workers and to promote the occupational risk.

item.title: Am I low on Vitamin D
item.description:
Free Test

4 ways to find out if you are low
Cost Time Result Description
FREE 1 minute <10ng press on breastbone and both shinbones
if 2 of 3 are painful then very low vitamin D
$10 1 month N.A. https://is.gd/60each50K Amazon
take one 50,000 IU daily for a week
See if you feel a benefit
If yes, take 1 capsule every 2 weeks
(very conservative, every easy, lowest cost)
$35 10 days <35 ng https://is.gd/dtestqual Amazon UK
2 testers which look similar to COVID tester
but uses 2 drops of blood instead of nasal mucus
yes/no answer in 10 minutes
$55 10 days quantitative
5 to 200 ng		 https://is.gd/vitdtestquant Amazon
Get results in <5 days

Details on Free Test

Press about 10 lbs with a thumb

item.title: 15 Signs of Vitamin D Deficiency - video
item.description:
• Video - Jan 2024
      • Chat-GPT on Vitamin D Deficiency - July 2023
      • 24+ Criteria to associate a health problem with low vitamin D

Video - Jan 2024

YouTube 7 minutes

01:04 ► Lack of Sun Exposure

01:25 ► Fatigue and Weakness

01:45 ► Bone and Joint Pain

02:05 ► Impaired Wound Healing

02:26 ► Muscle Pain and Weakness

item.title: Country noticed that their youths need more Vitamin D (Norway this time)
item.description:
• Vitamin D concentrations were often insufficient among native Norwegian adolescents and children with a non-Western immigrant background
      • Finland has learned to both fortify and supplement – and now may have the best vitamin D level on the planet

Vitamin D concentrations were often insufficient among native Norwegian adolescents and children with a non-Western immigrant background

Acta Paediatr 2024 Mar;113(3):411-416. doi: 10.1111/apa.17078

Chandra Sekhar Devulapalli 1

Aim: The aim of this study was to review papers that assessed the vitamin D intake of children living in Norway, as research has suggested that it has remained below the recommended daily allowance of 10 μg/day.

item.title: Ultra-processed food consumption: 2X increased risk of vitamin D deficiency
item.description:
• Consumption of ultra-processed products is associated with vitamin D deficiency in Brazilian adults and elderly
      • 4 of the many many possible pathways that could result in this observation
      • See also: Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies

Consumption of ultra-processed products is associated with vitamin D deficiency in Brazilian adults and elderly

Br J Nutr. 2023 Dec 28;130(12):2198-2205. doi: 10.1017/S000711452300154X PDF is behind a paywall

Larisse Monteles Nascimento 1, Layanne Cristina de Carvalho Lavôr 1, Paulo Víctor de Lima Sousa 1, Liania Alves Luzia 2, Poliana Cristina de Almeida Fonseca Viola 1, Adriana de Azevedo Paiva 1, Patricia Helen de Carvalho Rondó 2, Karoline de Macedo Gonçalves Frota 1

item.title: Nutrient consumption – only 7% of recommended DRV of Vitamin D (German Children in this case)
item.description:
• Unfavorable nutrient intakes in children up to school entry age: results from the nationwide German KiESEL study

Unfavorable nutrient intakes in children up to school entry age: results from the nationwide German KiESEL study

Front. Nutr., 23 January 2024 Volume 10 - 2023 | https://doi.org/10.3389/fnut.2023.1302323

Leonie Burgard1 Sara Jansen2 Clarissa Spiegler1 Anna-Kristin Brettschneider2 Andrea Straßburg1 Ute Alexy3 Stefan Storcksdieck genannt Bonsmann1 Regina Ensenauer2 Thorsten Heuer1*

1 Department of Nutritional Behaviour, Max Rubner-Institut (MRI) – Federal Research Institute of Nutrition and Food, Karlsruhe, Germany

item.title: Seniors need more Vitamin D (Geriatric Ward in this case)
item.description:
• Vitamin D Status among Patients Admitted to a Geriatric Ward-Are Recommendations for Preventing Its Deficiency Effective Enough?
      • VitaminDWiki – Elderly often trapped in vitamin D vicious circles

Vitamin D Status among Patients Admitted to a Geriatric Ward-Are Recommendations for Preventing Its Deficiency Effective Enough?

Nutrients 2024 Jan 6;16(2):193. doi: 10.3390/nu16020193.

Maksymilian Adam Lech 1, Marcin Warpechowski 1, Aleksandra Wojszel 1, Justyna Rentflejsz 2 3 4, Marta Świętek 4, Zyta Beata Wojszel 3 4

item.title: 14 Conservative Vitamin D recommendations in Ukraine
item.description:
• Ukrainian Consensus on Diagnosis and Management of Vitamin D Deficiency in Adults

Ukrainian Consensus on Diagnosis and Management of Vitamin D Deficiency in Adults

Nutrients 2024, 16, 270. https://doi.org/10.3390/ nu16020270

Nataliia Grygorieva * , Mykola Tronko 2, Volodymir Kovalenko 3, Serhiy Komisarenko 4 , Tetiana Tatarchuk 5, Ninel Dedukh 1, Mykola Veliky 4®, Serhiy Strafun 6C , Yulia Komisarenko 7C , Andrii Kalashnikov 6 , Valeria Orlenko 2, Volodymyr Pankiv 8 , Oleg Shvets 9 , Inna Gogunska 10 and Svitlana Regeda 11

item.title: Vitamin D recommended for infants by 98% of Belgian pediatricians - 2024
item.description:
• Self-reported prescribing behaviour of vitamin D prophylaxis in healthy children by Belgian paediatricians

Self-reported prescribing behaviour of vitamin D prophylaxis in healthy children by Belgian paediatricians

Eur J Clin Nutr. 2024 Jan 3. doi: 10.1038/s41430-023-01387-4

Charlotte De Crem 1, Myriam Van Winckel 2, Yvan Vandenplas 3, Anke Raaijmakers 4 5 6

There is currently no consensus on the guidelines for vitamin D prophylaxis in healthy children. The purpose of this study was to investigate the prescribing behaviour of vitamin D prophylaxis among Belgian paediatricians. Between June and September 2022, a questionnaire was distributed by email to all Belgian paediatricians who are a member of at least one of three scientific or professional organisations, as well as to the heads of every Belgian paediatric or neonatal hospital ward. We analysed 426 completed questionnaires. All regions, age categories and subspecialties were represented.

item.title: 98% of Vitamin D deficiency (8 Latin American countries)
item.description:
98% have less than the Estimated Average Requirement (E.A.R.) typically 400- 600 IU
80% Magnesium deficiency

      • A Landscape of Micronutrient Dietary Intake by 15- to 65-Years-Old Urban Population in 8 Latin American Countries: Results From the Latin American Study of Health and Nutrition

A Landscape of Micronutrient Dietary Intake by 15- to 65-Years-Old Urban Population in 8 Latin American Countries: Results From the Latin American Study of Health and Nutrition

DOI: 10.1177/03795721231215267

item.description:
Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki last updated Sept 2023

    • This is part of UpToDate by wolterskluwer.com
      • Table of contents - Vitamin D Deficiency - Patient education - Beyond the Basics
      • RELATED TOPICS

This is part of UpToDate by wolterskluwer.com

Table of contents - Vitamin D Deficiency - Patient education - Beyond the Basics

AUTHOR: Bess Dawson-Hughes, MD

IINTRODUCTION

WHAT IS VITAMIN D?

Natural sources of vitamin D

CAUSES OF VITAMIN D DEFICIENCY

item.title: Some seniors in Germany had vitamin D levels of 34 ng without supplementation (seems unlikely)
item.description:
• Folate, vitamin B12 and vitamin D status in healthy and active home-dwelling people over 70 years
      • The majority of Germans had Vitamin D levels < 30 ng in a previous study
      • See also Nursing homes and Vitamin D - many studies 24+ studies

Folate, vitamin B12 and vitamin D status in healthy and active home-dwelling people over 70 years

BMC Geriatr. 2023 Oct 18;23(1):673. doi: 10.1186/s12877-023-04391-2.

Felix Kerlikowsky 1, Jan Philipp Schuchardt 1, Andreas Hahn 2

item.title: Simple vitamin D supplementation - daily, weekly, or monthly
item.description:
• Supplementing Vitamin D in Different Patient Groups to Reduce Deficiency
      • VitaminDWiki - Vitamin D Conference - 6th Minimum, Maximum, Optimum - Sept 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - 13 reasons why many seniors need more vitamin D (both dose and level) - July 2023

Supplementing Vitamin D in Different Patient Groups to Reduce Deficiency

Nutrients 2023, 15(17), 3725; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu15173725

item.title: Vitamin D levels in a nursing home (Austria)
item.description:
• Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in seniors - A retrospective study
      • See also Nursing homes and Vitamin D - many studies 24+ studies

Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in seniors - A retrospective study

Clin Nutr ESPEN. 2023 Oct;57:691-696. doi: 10.1016/j.clnesp.2023.07.005

Stefan Haitchi 1, Paula Moliterno 2, Kurt Widhalm 3

Background & aims: Vitamin D deficiency is a condition with different causes. It is associated with numerous comorbidities such as autoimmune diseases, bone diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, neurodegenerative diseases, psychiatric diseases, and respiratory infections like COVID-19. Due to its high prevalence all over the world, it is a major task for health care systems worldwide. Through a combination of low sunlight exposure, insufficient nutrition, and age-related changes in skin, liver, and kidney function, especially seniors and nursing home residents, in particular, have a significantly increased risk of developing a vitamin D deficiency.

item.title: Seniors 5.4 more likely to die if Vitamin D less than 15 ng (Mexico)
item.description:
• Serum vitamin D levels and mortality in Mexicans: results from the Mexican Health and Aging Study
      • See also 13 reasons why many seniors need more vitamin D (both dose and level) - July 2023

Serum vitamin D levels and mortality in Mexicans: results from the Mexican Health and Aging Study

Nutr Hosp. 2023 Aug 28;40(4):732-738. doi: 10.20960/nh.04580.

item.title: Caffeine decreases Vitamin D in blood and cells - many studies
item.description:
• Caffeine deactivates Vitamin D Receptor gene (less D to cells) - Aug 2019
      • THE GENETICS OF OSTEOPOROSIS: Vitamin D Receptor Polymorphisms - July 1998
      • High Caffeine intake == > 1.5X more likely to be Vitamin D Deficient - Sept 2021
      • The effects of caffeine on bone mineral density and fracture risk - Jan 2022
      • VitaminDWiki – Vitamin D Receptor is associated in over 58 autoimmune studies

Caffeine deactivates Vitamin D Receptor gene (less D to cells) - Aug 2019

The Effect of Caffeine on Calcitriol-Inducible Vitamin D Receptor-Controlled Gene Expression in Intestinal and Osteoblastic Cells

item.title: Signs of Vitamin D Deficiency - many studies
item.description:
• An AI summary (by Claude) of the top 5 signs of Vitamin D Deficiency
      • Chat-GPT - July 2023
      • See related in VitaminDWiki

An AI summary (by Claude) of the top 5 signs of Vitamin D Deficiency

Claude 2, July 2023

Please describe the top 5 signs of Vitamin D deficiency - you can use the attached PDFs

Based on the attached PDF articles, here are the top 5 signs of vitamin D deficiency:

item.title: Respiratory infection, children and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• RTI Meta-analysis failed to look at any Vitamin D dosing >2,000 IU - July 2023
      • Acute respiratory infections decreased a small amount when a small amount of vitamin D was added – meta-analysis March 2020

RTI Meta-analysis failed to look at any Vitamin D dosing >2,000 IU - July 2023

A meta-analysis of the association between vitamin D supplementation and the risk of acute respiratory tract infection in the healthy pediatric group

item.title: Depressed infants have very low Vitamin D (7.5 ng)
item.description:
• Serum Vitamin D status in full-term neonates with severe perinatal depression

Serum Vitamin D status in full-term neonates with severe perinatal depression

J Neonatal Perinatal Med . 2023;16(2):257-264. doi: 10.3233/NPM-230020 PDF is behind a $27 paywall

P Batra 1, P Singh 1, R S Ahmed 2, D Harit 1

Background: Severe perinatal depression is a significant cause of mortality and morbidity in neonates. Vitamin D levels were observed to be low in mothers and their neonates with hypoxic ischemic encephalopathy in some studies, owing to its neuroprotective properties.

item.title: COVID lockdowns reduced Vitamin D levels - should supplement
item.description:
• Influence of Strict Lockdown on Vitamin D Deficiency in Pregnant Women: A Word of Caution
      • See also VitaminDWiki

Influence of Strict Lockdown on Vitamin D Deficiency in Pregnant Women: A Word of Caution

Nutrients. 2023 Apr 19;15(8):1972. doi: 10.3390/nu15081972.Nuria Agüero-Domenech 1 2, Eduardo Bernabeu 3 4, Antonio García-Valentín 3, Ana Sarrión 1, Silvia Jover 1, Javier Baranda 1, Ernesto Cortés-Castell 5, Vicente Gil-Guillén 6, María J García-Teruel 1 2

>75% of pregnant women were deficient (<20 ng) duing strict lockdown

Black bar = % <20 ng/mL

item.title: 94 percent of children with Chronic Neurological Diseases had low vitamin D
item.description:
• Vitamin D Insufficiency in Children with Chronic Neurological Diseases: Frequency and Causative Factors
      • VitaminDWiki – Cerebral Palsy and Vitamin D - many studies

Vitamin D Insufficiency in Children with Chronic Neurological Diseases: Frequency and Causative Factors

Iran J Child Neurol . 2023 Spring;17(2):31-38. doi: 10.22037/ijcn.v17i2.35938

Gunes Isik 1, Bilal Ustundag 2, Yasar Dogan 3

Objective: Vitamin D insufficiency/rickets is a metabolic bone disease that leads to insufficient mineralization of bone. Chronic neurological diseases, including cerebral palsy (CP), convulsive disorders, neural tube defects, myopathy, immobility, lack of sun exposure, inadequate nutrition, and antiepileptic drugs (AEDs) can cause vitamin D insufficiency and osteopenia in children.

item.title: 1 in 5 Canadians have inadequate Vitamin D (less than 16 ng), far more if dark skinned – Canada
item.description:
• Vitamin D Status of People 3 to 79 Years of Age from the Canadian Health Measures Survey 2012-2019
      • VitaminDWiki – Is 50 ng of vitamin D too high, just right, or not enough

Vitamin D Status of People 3 to 79 Years of Age from the Canadian Health Measures Survey 2012-2019

J Nutr. 2023 Apr;153(4):1150-1161. doi: 10.1016/j.tjnut.2023.02.026

item.title: 6 billion people have less than 75 nmol (30 ng) of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Global and regional prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in population-based studies from 2000 to 2022: A pooled analysis of 7.9 million participants
      • VitaminDWiki – Is 50 ng of vitamin D too high, just right, or not enough
      • VitaminDWiki –Maps of deficiency in 2014 (<75 nmol = grey)

Global and regional prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in population-based studies from 2000 to 2022: A pooled analysis of 7.9 million participants

Front. Nutr., 17 March 2023 Sec. Nutritional Epidemiology Volume 10 - 2023 https://doi.org/10.3389/fnut.2023.1070808

item.title: Test of count of items in deficiency category
10 Groups Are More Prone To Vitamin D Deficiency - 2017
item.description:
• These 10 Groups Of People Are More Prone To Vitamin D Deficiency
      • See also VitaminDWiki

These 10 Groups Of People Are More Prone To Vitamin D Deficiency

Prevention Magazine Sept 2017

Lots of details in the article

  1. Adults over 55 (less D generated by the sun)

  2. Office workers

  3. People with darker skin

  4. Inflammatory bowel disease patients

item.title: Vitamin D Deficiency - 14 signs
item.description:
• 14 Signs of Vitamin D Deficiency - Discover Magazine

14 Signs of Vitamin D Deficiency - Discover Magazine

Magazine has a short description of each sign

  1. Aching Muscles

  2. Painful Bones

  3. Fatigue

  4. Reduced Endurance

  5. Low Moods

  6. Problems Sleeping Well

  7. Sweaty Head

  8. Losing Hair

  9. Wounds Heal Slowly

  10. Dizziness

  11. Heart problems

  12. Weight gain

  13. Recurring infections

  14. Reduced Cognitive Function

item.title: Vitamin D Deficiency in South America - meta-analysis
item.description:
• Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in South America: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Prevalence of vitamin D deficiency in South America: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Nutr Rev . 2023 Mar 7;nuad010. doi: 10.1093/nutrit/nuad010   PDF is behind a $39 paywall

Marcela M Mendes 1, Anna P O Gomes 2, Maísa M Araújo 1, Alexandre S G Coelho 3, Kênia M B Carvalho 1, Patrícia B Botelho 1

Context: Vitamin D deficiency is considered a global public health issue, even in low-latitude countries where there is abundant sunlight radiation. Nevertheless, the prevalence of vitamin D insufficiency and deficiency in the South American continent has not been well characterized.
